blockingIssues.description: applyZAIFileAPIKeyAttributes sets auth_kind=apikey and default base_url for provider zai but does not skip or disable auth records when api_key/api-key is absent or blank. Git, object, and Postgres store readers call this helper and can append active routable auth records without API keys, leaving VAL-ZAI-004 unmet.

The SDK file token store has a duplicate Z.AI file-auth attribute helper with the same missing/blank API-key behavior, leaving active zai auth records with auth_kind=apikey and default base_url but no api_key attribute.

The non-streaming and streaming execution paths only add Authorization when apiKey is non-empty and otherwise continue to POST upstream, so an active auth record without an API key is not rejected before execution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a Z.AI auth-file hardening note when touching file-backed credentials: keep watcher synthesis, sdk/auth filestore loading, internal/store file/git/object/postgres paths, and executor credential validation consistent for missing or blank API keys.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: zai-config-auth-hardening hardened internal/watcher/synthesizer/file.go but left internal/store/zai_auth.go, sdk/auth/filestore.go, and executor empty-key handling able to produce or use active Z.AI auth records without API keys.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Keep or strengthen the EndFeatureRun reminder that successful handoff requires commitId as a top-level field, and consider validating this before workers attempt handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The zai-config-auth-hardening handoff recorded an orchestrator repair after repeated EndFeatureRun failures caused by omitting the required top-level commitId.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Ensure completedWorkerSessionId entries are present in worker-transcripts.jsonl or record where their transcript skeletons can be found before scrutiny validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The assigned completed session e42ed717-549d-420d-970b-edd488e9a789 for zai-config-auth-hardening was absent from worker-transcripts.jsonl, preventing the reviewer from verifying that exact transcript skeleton.
